name: Trinity_Ternary_CPU_M1Pro
author: gHashTag
val_bpb_note: Single seed 42, trained 24h on Apple M1 Pro CPU-only. Architecture: 10L × 512d × 8h, 24M params, 47% ternary QAT blend reached. Artifact 5.59 MB LZMA-compressed (base-3 ternary packing).

val_bpb_alpha_configs.note: Best val_bpb at the trained ternary blend (47%). Full ternary (alpha=1.0) degrades significantly — 24h CPU budget insufficient to reach 100% ternary convergence. Further training would close this gap (BitNet b1.58 recipes typically need several days on GPU to fully ternarize a model of this size).

compliance.hardware: Apple MacBook Pro 18,3 — M1 Pro CPU (10 cores, 16 GB RAM)

description: First Parameter Golf submission trained entirely on Apple M1 Pro CPU (no GPU, no MPS, no CUDA). BitNet b1.58-style ternary QAT with straight-through estimator. Weights packed via Trinity base-3 encoding (5 trits per byte, 99.06% of theoretical optimum). LZMA-compressed artifact 5.59 MB — leaves 10.4 MB headroom for larger models at future runs.
base: Trinity framework (github.com/gHashTag/trinity) + BitNet b1.58 + Parameter Golf SP1024 pipeline
architecture: 10L × 512d × 8h/8kv MLP 2.5× ReLU² RoPE, RMSNorm, tied embeddings, softcap 30

training.hardware: Apple M1 Pro, 10 cores CPU, 16 GB RAM
training.ternary_schedule: fp32 warmup 500 steps → linear ramp to alpha=1.0 over remaining steps (reached alpha=0.47 at 24h cutoff)

techniques: Trinity base-3 packing (5 trits/byte, 1.6 bits/trit, 99% of log₂(3) optimum)
Linear ternary ramp schedule (smooth fp32→ternary transition)
Apple Silicon AMX (Apple Matrix Extensions) for efficient CPU matmul
notable_facts: 🍎 First Parameter Golf submission on Apple Silicon CPU-only (no GPU, no MPS, no CUDA)
🔺 Ternary QAT from scratch (BitNet b1.58 recipe)
📦 5.59 MB compressed artifact = 10.4 MB headroom under 16 MB limit
🏠 Fully reproducible on consumer laptop (16 GB RAM), no cloud required
🔬 Research demo: ternary QAT needs >24h CPU to fully ternarize this model size — longer runs expected to close alpha=1.0 gap
⚡ 4006 tokens/sec throughput on M1 Pro (10 cores)
